draft: Sketch the ERD on paper first, the way the exam expects: three boxes, the associative entity in the middle, crow's-foot cardinality on each line, and solid (identifying) vs. dashed (non-identifying) lines. Then transfer it into the fields below.

fields.label: An employee can work on many projects, and a project can have many employees. What is the cardinality of the employee–project relationship?
Mark each foreign-key relationship as identifying or non-identifying.
fields.help: Ask the one-question test: is the parent's key part of the child's primary key?

fields.label: Resolve the employee–project many-to-many into an associative entity. Model exactly three entities and the two relationships.
fields.help: Give employee a PK employee_id, project a PK project_id, and assignment the composite PK (employee_id, project_id). Then add employee→assignment and project→assignment as 1:N identifying relationships.
